name: GPT-5.5 (xHigh)
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; 'xhigh' (maximum) reasoning-effort configuration
note: Highest reasoning-effort sibling of the base 'GPT-5.5' entry ('reserved for the hardest quality-first workloads' per OpenAI's own guidance). Same weights/pricing/context as the default and High siblings; distinguished by reasoning.effort=xhigh.

results.notes: Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier1-3 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.
Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier4 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Codex harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 26.6% / Score 47.9% (rank 10). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('xHigh'). Score component used per corpus convention.

Read by exact rank-position matching against AA's live ranked benchmark chart (GPT-5.5 (xhigh) placed between Claude Opus 4.7 (max) and GPT-5.5 (high), both at 50%). verificationNotes:
Confirmed as a distinct, independently-benchmarked configuration on Artificial Analysis (model page 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', explicitly labeled 'Reasoning Effort Label: xhigh' on direct fetch; Intelligence Index score 55, rank #7/188, strictly ahead of the 'high' sibling's 53/#12). benchlm.ai's benchmark table for GPT-5.5 explicitly attributes its FrontierMath v2 figures to 'xhigh effort' (Tier 1-3: 51.7%, Tier 4: 35.4%) — these are numerically identical to the FrontierMath scores already stored on the pre-existing base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry, strongly suggesting that entry's headline benchmark numbers are actually xhigh-tier results mislabeled as the plain/default config. Flagging for the ingesting pipeline to consider migrating those two FrontierMath results (and possibly GDPval/BrowseComp/SWE-Bench Pro/Terminal-Bench, which are likely also xhigh headline figures) from the base entry onto this xHigh entry, and to set the base entry's isBestConfig to false since this sibling is now the true best-of-cohort.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark completeness check (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu): all seven resolve to omitted_no_evidence -- no citable xhigh-specific (or even effort-unspecified) score exists for any of them. (1) Official materials: three independent close-readings of openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5 (kingy.ai's '9 numbers quoted verbatim from OpenAI's evaluation tables', vellum.ai's 16-benchmark comprehensive listing, and alexlavaee.me's review) all enumerate OpenAI's launch benchmarks in full and none lists MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, or MMMU -- OpenAI's own launch page evidently never reported these seven. (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis maintains dedicated AIME-2025, LiveCodeBench, and MATH-500 leaderboards but each is capped at a static 7-of-269/343/201 'spotlight' model set that excludes every GPT-5.5 variant entirely -- AA has not run these three evals on GPT-5.5 at all. AA's own 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' model page (fetched live) lists its full tracked-eval set (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, MMMU-Pro) and it contains no MMLU, base MMMU, HumanEval, or SWE-bench (classic/Verified) entry -- AA has fully retired these in favor of harder successors (MMLU-Pro, MMMU-Pro, SWE-bench Pro, etc.) for frontier-tier models by mid-2026. (3) Sibling cross-reference: neither the base 'GPT-5.5' nor 'GPT-5.5 (High)' corpus entries carry any of these seven benchmarks either, a consistent absence across the whole family already vetted in prior rounds. One contested exception was investigated and rejected: TokenMix.ai and marc0.dev both circulate an unsourced 'SWE-Bench Verified 88.7% / MMLU 92.4%' pair attributed loosely to 'OpenAI announcement', but this pair appears nowhere in the three careful launch-post transcriptions above, is not effort-specific, and could not be corroborated on OpenAI's own page (blocked from direct fetch) or on Vals.ai/epoch.ai (access blocked/no data returned) -- treated as unreliable and not added. This matches the taxonomy's own framing of mmlu ('largely saturated at the frontier') and humaneval ('long-saturated at the frontier') as benchmarks frontier labs are expected to stop publishing.
